functions.name: CNContainer.Name
functions.description: The name of the container. The container is the source of contacts, e.g. an account on iCloud.

parameters.name: ContainerIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the container.

examples.label: Query names of containers:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of container IDs
	list = MBS( "CNContactStore.Containers" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "CNContainer.Name"; id); 
	names = names & id & " -> " &name & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContainer.Type
functions.description: Queries type of container. Type can be Unassigned, Local, Exchange or CardDAV.

parameters.name: ContainerIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the container.

examples.label: Queries container types:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of container IDs
	list = MBS( "CNContactStore.Containers" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "CNContainer.Name"; id); 
	type = MBS( "CNContainer.Type"; id); 
	names = names & name & " -> " & type & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IKSlideShow.Add
functions.description: Adds a slide. The container can contain an image (GIF, PNG, BMP, JPEG) or an image file (TIFF, BMP, PNG, JPEG and other formats supported by macOS) or a PDF file. For PDF files each page is made one slide. This is the general function. It may often be better to use the functions IKSlideShow.AddImage , IKSlideShow.AddPath , IKSlideShow.AddPDF or IKSlideShow.AddURL . Sets mode to images.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container with the image or the file.

functions.result: Returns the number of slides added or an error message.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.AddImage
functions.description: Adds a slide. The container can contain an image (GIF, PNG, BMP, JPEG) or an image file (TIFF, BMP, PNG, JPEG and other formats supported by macOS). Sets mode to images.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image as container.

functions.result: Returns the number of slides added or an error message.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.AddPDF
functions.description: Adds a slide. Each page is made one slide. You can pass a container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open . Sets mode to PDF.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF.

functions.result: Returns the number of slides added or an error message.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.AddPath
functions.description: Adds a slide. The file can contain an image (GIF, PNG, BMP, JPEG) or an image file (TIFF, BMP, PNG, JPEG and other formats supported by macOS) Sets mode to images. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the image file.

functions.result: Returns the number of slides added or an error message.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.AddURL
functions.description: Adds a slide. The URL can be http/https and some other formats Apple's frameworks know about. Sets mode to images.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The file url to the image.

functions.result: Returns the number of slides added or an error message.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.Clear
functions.description: Clears the list of slides.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the item to remove. From 0 to IKSlideShow.Count -1. If parameter is not provided, we clear all items.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.Count
functions.description: Returns number of items.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetAudioFile
functions.description: Queries the audio file path.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetMode
functions.description: Queries the mode for slideshow. A slideshow is in one of three modes. If used with the wrong mode, you are in trouble and the framework can crash.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetPDFDisplayBox
functions.description: Queries the current PDF display box. possible values: kPDFDisplayBoxMediaBox = 0 kPDFDisplayBoxCropBox = 1 kPDFDisplayBoxBleedBox = 2 kPDFDisplayBoxTrimBox = 3 kPDFDisplayBoxArtBox = 4

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetPDFDisplayMode
functions.description: Queries the current PDF display mode. Display modes: kPDFDisplaySinglePage = 0 kPDFDisplaySinglePageContinuous = 1 kPDFDisplayTwoUp = 2 kPDFDisplayTwoUpContinuous = 3

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetPDFDisplaysAsBook
functions.description: Queries whether PDFs are displayed as book currently.
functions.result: Returns the current value as boolean.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetScreen
functions.description: Queries screen index. -1 is default while 0 to Screen.Count -1 is an index to a screen.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetStartIndex
functions.description: Queries the start index for the slide show. The index is 0 based. -1 is the default start index.
functions.result: Returns the index of the first slide.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetStartPaused
functions.description: Queries whether the slide show should start paused. Default value is false.
functions.result: Returns the current value for the start pause setting.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.GetWrapAround
functions.description: Queries the current wrap around setting.
functions.result: a boolean value whether the slide show wraps around.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.ItemAtIndex
functions.description: Queries item at index.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: From 0 to IKSlideShow.Count -1.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: For PDF or image files returned an optional file name.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: For images the type you like to get. Can be JPEG, TIFF, PNG, GIF, BMP or PDF.

examples.label: Add an item and query it back:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
IKSlideShow.AddURL
"; "http://www.mbsplugins.de/images/xdc2016.jpg" )]
Show Custom Dialog ["Count";
MBS
( "
IKSlideShow.Count
" )]
Show Custom Dialog ["First item";
MBS
( "IKSlideShow.ItemAtIndex"; 0 )]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IKSlideShow.AddURL"; "http://www.mbsplugins.de/images/xdc2016.jpg" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Count"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IKSlideShow.Count" )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"First item"; MBS( "IKSlideShow.ItemAtIndex"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>0 )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IKSlideShow.Run
functions.description: Runs the slide show with the current slide list and the current options.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the audio file.

functions.result: display mode	The new display mode value.

functions.name: IKSlideShow.SetWrapAround

functions.name: Modbus.CalculateRTUMessageCRC
functions.description: Calculates the RTU Message CRC string.

MBS("Modbus.CalculateRTUMessageCRC"; "010400010001")</Calculation>

functions.name: Notification.Listen
functions.description: Starts listening for a notification. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a notification is received. The script parameter will be the notification name plus parameters. Although the notification name can be any text, you probably want to use reverse dns notation to avoid conflicts. This is implemented with Distributed Notifications on Mac and named messaged on Windows. You can use our NSDistributedNotificationCenterMBS and WinNotificationMBS classes in the Real Studio plugins to send/receive this notifications. Version 7.2 and newer pass parameters to the script call. On Windows you get both parameters (WParam and LParam). For version 7.3 you can receive a payload in json as script parameter to the script call after the name of notification. If you need to trigger scripts on other computers, please check UDPSocket or ServerSocket functions. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: NotificationName
parameters.description: The name of the notification.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers script event:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Notification.Listen"; "de.mbsplugins.MyTestNotification"; Get ( FileName ); "GotTestNotification")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Listen for device change event on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Notification.Listen"; 537; Get ( FileName ); "DeviceChanged")

// Notifies an application of a change to the hardware configuration of a device or the computer.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Listen for System time change event on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Notification.Listen"; 31; Get ( FileName ); "TimeChangedTrigger")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Listen for display change on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Notification.Listen"; 126; Get ( FileName ); "DisplayChangedTrigger")

// The WM_DISPLAYCHANGE message is sent to all windows when the display resolution has changed.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Notification.Send
functions.description: Sends a notification. This notification is delivered to all applications on the computer which register for receiving it.

parameters.name: NotificationName
parameters.description: The name of the notification.

parameters.name: Param1
parameters.description: A number to pass on Windows. For Mac in 7.3 plugins you can pass JSON here.

parameters.name: Param2
parameters.description: A number to pass on Windows.

examples.label: Send notification on Mac with JSON payload:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Notification.Send"; "MyNotification"; "{\"recordID\":5}")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Notification.SendDirect
functions.description: Sends a notification within FileMaker to all listener. This notification is delivered to all listening scripts within the FileMaker instance. For debugging.

parameters.name: NotificationName
parameters.description: The name of the notification.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter. If you don't specify, we pass name of notifcation as usual. By passing your own parameter, you can include several values like a list of values including record IDs or reference numbers.

functions.name: Notification.Unlisten
functions.description: Unregisters a notification handler.

parameters.name: NotificationName
parameters.description: The name of the notification.

functions.name: Notification.UnlistenAll
functions.description: Stops listening to all notifications.

parameters.name: Key

functions.name: PrintDialog.Enable
functions.description: Enables the print dialog extension. Your print commands in FileMaker should show a dialog, so the plugin can intercept it. If you disable dialog for the FileMake Script step, the plugin has no way to intercept the printing. Calls PrintDialog.Install internally if needed.

examples.label: Install and print to PDF:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.Install
")]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "PrintDialog.Enable")]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.SetPrintToPDF
"; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.SetDestinationPath
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pdf")]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.SetNoDialog
"; 1 )]
Print []
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.Install")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.Enable")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.SetPrintToPDF"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.SetDestinationPath"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pdf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.SetNoDialog"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="43" name="Print">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetAllPages
functions.description: Queries whether to print all pages. Value is -1 if not defined.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetConfiguration
functions.description: Queries configuration from last print. You can run with dialog, select all settings you like and then store the configuration. Configuration is the serialized and base64 encoded setup data. Just store as it is in a text field for later restoration with PrintDialog.SetConfiguration or PDFKit.SavePrintSettings . Returns empty result if no print configuration was yet created. See PrintDialog.GetLastSettings for a xml you can read.

examples.label: Query configuration after printing:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PrintDialog.Enable
") ]
Print [ With dialog: On ]
Set Field [
Printer Dialog on Mac::Data
;
MBS
("PrintDialog.GetConfiguration") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PrintDialog.Enable") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="43" name="Print">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PrintDialog.GetConfiguration") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Printer Dialog on Mac" id="99999" name="Data "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetCopies
functions.description: Queries number of copies. This property is the number of copies of the print job to be printed. Value is -1 if not defined.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetDestinationPath
functions.description: Queries the destination path.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetDuplex
functions.description: Queries the duplex mode. Use PrintDialog.SetDuplex to set this value. Setting is 1 (on), 0 (off) or -1 (not set).

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetFirstPage
functions.description: Queries first page. This property containing the one-based index of the first page in the job to print. Value is -1 if not defined.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetLastPage
functions.description: Queries last page. This property containing the one-based index of the last page in the job to print. Value is -1 if not defined.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetLastSettings
functions.description: Queries last settings from print dialog. Please print once with enabled print dialog functions. After printing with dialog, you can query last settings and see what OS X or your driver uses. Since version 10.1 we return settings as JSON instead of XML and includes print parameter.

examples.label: Get last settings:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query settings after using dialog:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PrintDialog.Install
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PrintDialog.Enable
") ]
Print [ With dialog: On ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PrintDialog.Disable
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PrintDialog.GetLastSettings") ]
Set Field [
Printer Dialog on Mac::Path
;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PrintDialog.Install") </Calculation>

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetNoDialog
functions.description: Queries whether dialog shows. Use PrintDialog.SetNoDialog to set this setting.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetOption
functions.description: Queries an option. You can use PrintDialog.SetOption to set options or PrintDialog.ClearOption to clear one.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetPageNumberingOffset
functions.description: Queries page numbering offset. Use PrintDialog.SetPageNumberingOffset to set this value. Value -1 means to not change it. Value 0 is for no offset. Other values to start page numbering at given number.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetPrintToPDF
functions.description: Queries whether printing is going to PDF file.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetPrintType
functions.description: Queries print type. You get the value set by PrintDialog.SetPrintType returned. Value -1 means the plugin doesn't change the setting.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetPrinterName
functions.description: Queries printer name to use. You can set this with PrintDialog.SetPrinterName .

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetReset
functions.description: Query whether to reset print settings.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetResetDestination
functions.description: Queries whether ResetDestination option is set.

functions.name: PrintDialog.GetReverseOrder
functions.description: Queries reverse order setting. Returns 1 if reverse order, 0 if normal and -1 if not defined.

examples.label: Try functions:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.SetReverseOrder
"; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PrintDialog.GetReverseOrder" ) ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.Install
functions.description: Installs the extension for the printer dialog. Only after PrintDialog.Install was called, you can use PrintDialog.Enable . You only call this function once. More calls are ignored. This is Mac only. For Windows, please check Printer.SetPrinter and Printer.Print .

PrintDialog.SetDestinationPath

PrintDialog.SetNoDialog

functions.name: PrintDialog.Reset
functions.description: Resets settings in plugin. We reset our properties back to defaults. Copies, AllPages, FirstPage, LastPage, Orientation, Duplex, PageNumberingOffset, PrintStyleSetting, PrintTypePopupIndex, PrintTypeRadioIndex, ReverseOrder, PaperWidth, PaperHeight are all set to -1. NoDialog, PrintToPDF, Reset and FixDestination is set to off. PrinterName, PaperName, Options, LastSettings, DestinationPath, Tray and LoadedSettings are cleared.

examples.label: Sets printer name, resets and checks:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
r1 =
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.SetPrinterName
"; "Hello" );
r2 =
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.GetPrinterName
" );
r3 =
MBS
( "PrintDialog.Reset"; "Hello" );
r4 =
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.GetPrinterName
" )
]; r2 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
r1 = MBS( "PrintDialog.SetPrinterName"; "Hello" );
r2 = MBS( "PrintDialog.GetPrinterName" );
r3 = MBS( "PrintDialog.Reset"; "Hello" );
r4 = MBS( "PrintDialog.GetPrinterName" )
]; r2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.SetOption
functions.description: Sets an option. You can use PrintDialog.GetLastSettings after printing to query XML with all settings and pick keys and values. When you are done after printing, you may use PrintDialog.ClearOptions to clear options for next print.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to use. Depending on data type, the plugin will pass a number or a text.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of data to add. Default is empty to use type of value passed. So text or number type would be picked automatically. Valid values for type are text, number, integer and text. Integer is for integer numbers while number is for floating numbers.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.SetReset
functions.description: Sets whether to reset print settings. When this is set to 1, we reset print settings to the default ones. This removes any settings including presets.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value. Pass 1 or 0.

functions.name: PrintDialog.SetResetDestination
functions.description: Sets whether to fix destination. FileMaker 15 and older on macOS Sierra show a problem with printing. If you once print to PDF, the print dialog in FileMaker will remember the PDF printing and even print to PDF if no PDF printing is selected. If you set this option to 1, the plugin will reset the print destination to the printer before the dialog is shown or other options are applied.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable reset of destination. Pass 0 to disable.

examples.label: Initialize to fix printing issues with FileMaker and macOS Sierra:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.Install
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PrintDialog.Enable
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PrintDialog.SetResetDestination"; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.Install" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.Enable" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PrintDialog.SetResetDestination"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PrintDialog.SetTray
functions.description: Sets the tray name the plugin should select. Value may be Tray1, Tray2, tray-1, tray-2, auto, Left, Right, Lower, Middle, Upper and depends on the printer driver. This is not the name displayed in dialog, but the internal value which is the same for all localization. Same as using PrintDialog.SetOption with "InputSlot" as key and tray name as value. Please print once with dialog to pick a tray and then query PrintDialog.GetLastSettings to lookup the internal tray name. Usually named InputSlot in the settings. Warning: For some users the dialog loads last settings and overwrite this settings, so you may need to not show dialog.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The name of the tray to use.

examples.label: Set Tray-2 as the tray for paper:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PrintDialog.SetTray"; "Tray-2")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Name

functions.name: Saxon.IsLicensed
functions.description: Whether the license for Saxon is set.

examples.label: Check license:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("Saxon.IsLicensed") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "
MBS
License with Saxon" ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "
MBS
License without Saxon" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("Saxon.IsLicensed") ]
	Show Custom Dialog [ "MBS License with Saxon" ]
Else
	Show Custom Dialog [ "MBS License without Saxon" ]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Saxon.IsLoaded
functions.description: Whether the library has been loaded. Returns 1 if loaded or 0 if not yet loaded.

examples.label: Check whether library is loaded:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("Saxon.IsLoaded") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Library loaded?" ; "Saxon library loaded: " &
MBS
("
Saxon.Version
") ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Library loaded?" ; "Saxon library not loaded" ]
End If

	Show Custom Dialog [ "Library loaded?" ; "Saxon library loaded: " & MBS("Saxon.Version") ]
Else
	Show Custom Dialog [ "Library loaded?" ; "Saxon library not loaded" ]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>

functions.name: Saxon.Validate
functions.description: Validates a XML against the given schema. Validate refers to the process of checking whether an XML document conforms to a defined structure and rules specified in a schema (like XSD) or DTD. This ensures the XML data is both well-formed (syntactically correct) and valid (logically structured as expected). Set the current working directory with Saxon.SetCWD function, so the function can find relative files. Requires the Saxon-EEV license. The validation mode may be either strict or lax. The default is strict; the lax parameter may be called to indicate that lax validation is required. With strict validation, validation fails if no element declaration can be located for the outermost element. With lax validation, the absence of an element declaration results in the content being considered valid.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to work on.

parameters.name: Schema
parameters.description: The schema to use for validation.

parameters.name: lax
parameters.description: Whether to use lax rules.

parameters.name: xsdversion
parameters.description: The XSD Version to use.

examples.label: Try validation:
examples.text_code: MBS ( "Saxon.Validate"; "12"; "" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Saxon.Validate"; "<request><a>1</a><b>2</b></request>"; "<xs:schema xmlns:xs='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema' ¶
elementFormDefault='qualified' ¶
attributeFormDefault='unqualified'><xs:element ¶
name='request'><xs:complexType><xs:sequence><xs:element name='a' ¶
type='xs:string'/><xs:element name='b' ¶
type='xs:string'/></xs:sequence><xs:assert test='count(child::node()) = ¶
2'/></xs:complexType></xs:element></xs:schema>" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Saxon.ValidationReport
functions.description: Queries the last validation report.

examples.label: Run validation:

Set Variable [
$Schema
; Value:
Saxon XSLT::Schema
]
Set Variable [
$Schema
; Value: Substitute(
$Schema
; Char(13); Char(10)) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Saxon.Validate
";
Saxon XSLT::XML
;
$Schema
; 2 ) ]
Set Field [
Saxon XSLT::Result
;
$r
]
Set Field [
Saxon XSLT::Messages
;
MBS
("Saxon.ValidationReport") ]

    </Value>

    </Value>

functions.name: Saxon.XPathQuery
functions.description: Runs a XPath query. XPathQuery (commonly called XPath) is a language used to navigate and select nodes from an XML document using path-like expressions. It's often used within XSLT, XQuery, and XML-processing tools to pinpoint data locations efficiently. You can set the language version with Saxon.SetLanguageVersion function. Set the current working directory with Saxon.SetCWD function, so the function can find relative files. XPath 3.1 Basic Provides all XPath 3.1 features which do not require schema-awareness or higher-order functions. This includes an implementation of maps and arrays, and support for JSON, as well as language constructs retained from earlier XPath versions. XPath 3.1 Higher-Order Provides higher-order functions: specifically, the ability to use functions as values, including dynamic function calls, inline functions, partial function application, and a library of built-in higher-order functions. XPath 3.1 Schema Aware with EE-V license Provides schema-awareness: specifically, any use of source documents with type annotations, and any use of XPath expressions that contain the names of schema components such as element declarations and types, other than the built-in types.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to work on.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query to run. XPath query syntax uses path-like expressions to navigate through elements and attributes in an XML document. It starts with / for absolute paths (from the root) or // for relative paths (anywhere in the document). Nodes are selected by names (e.g., /bookstore/book), and filters can be applied using square brackets (e.g., //book[price>30]). You can also use @ to select attributes (e.g., //book[@category='fiction']) and functions like text(), contains(), or position() to refine queries.

parameters.name: Namespaces
parameters.description: The list of namespaces to declare in the style Key=URL.

parameters.name: VariableList
parameters.description: List of parameters to become variables.

examples.label: Run query:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Saxon.XPathQuery"; 
"<out><person>text1</person><person>text2</person><person>text3</person></out>"; 
"//person[1]")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run with two variables:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Saxon.Clear
") ]
# put in the value for the variable
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Saxon.SetParameter
"; "param"; "1"; "integer") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Saxon.SetParameter
"; "other"; "
2.4
"; "json") ]
# now run a query and pass list of variables
Set Variable [
$query
; Value: "
$param
+
$other
" ]
Set Variable [
$VariableList
; Value: "param¶other" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Saxon.XPathQuery"; "<test/>";
$Query
;
$VariableList
) ]
Set Field [
Saxon XSLT::Result
;
$r
]

  </Step>

    </Value>

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>"$param+$other" </Calculation>
    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$query</Name>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>"param¶other" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$VariableList</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: Saxon.XQuery
functions.description: Runs a XML Query. XQuery is a powerful query language designed to extract and manipulate data from XML documents. It can perform complex operations such as filtering, joining, and transforming XML data much like SQL does for relational databases. You can set the language version with Saxon.SetLanguageVersion function. Set the current working directory with Saxon.SetCWD function, so the function can find relative files. The query BaseURI can be set with Saxon.SetBaseURI function. XQuery includes: XQuery 3.1 Minimal Conformance: Provides Minimal Conformance (including try/catch and "group-by", as well as language features retained from earlier XQuery versions) as defined in section 5 of the XQuery 3.1 specification. XQuery 3.1 Modules: Provides the Module feature, which allows a query to be made up of multiple modules. XQuery 3.1 Serialization: Provides the Serialization feature. This allows the output of a query to be serialized as lexical XML, or in other formats including HTML, JSON, and plain text, under the control of serialization parameters contained either in the query itself, or supplied externally. XQuery 3.1 Higher-Order: Provides the Higher-Order Function feature. This provides the ability to use functions as values, including dynamic function calls, inline functions, partial function application, and a library of built-in higher-order functions. Streaming with EE license: Provides saxon:stream(), an extension function to allow large documents to be processed without holding the whole document in memory.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to work on.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query to run. XQuery syntax is designed to query and transform XML data using FLWOR expressions—For, Let, Where, Order by, and Return. It allows variables (prefixed with $), path expressions (like XPath), conditional logic (if, where), and supports functions to manipulate XML or return results in various formats. XQuery builds on XPath, so all XPath expressions are valid inside XQuery.

parameters.name: Namespaces
parameters.description: The list of namespaces to declare in the style Key=URL.

functions.name: Saxon.XSLT
functions.description: Runs a XML stylesheet transformation. XSLT (Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations) is a language used to transform XML documents into other formats like HTML, plain text, or another XML structure. It uses templates and rules to match XML elements and apply transformations, often guided by XPath expressions. Set the current working directory with Saxon.SetCWD function, so the function can find relative files. The output BaseURI can be set with Saxon.SetBaseURI function. XSLT (Transformation Processing) includes: XSLT 3.0 Basic Processor: Provides all mandatory features from the XSLT 3.0 specification (including try/ catch, iterate, accumulators, maps, named modes, content value templates, and extended patterns, as well as features retained from XSLT 2.0). XSLT 3.0 Serialization: Provides the serialization feature: specifically, the ability to convert the result trees produced as output of an XSLT transformation to lexical XML, or other formats including HTML, JSON, and plain text, under the control of serialization parameters defined in the stylesheet or via an external API. XSLT 3.0 Compatibility: Provides XSLT 1.0 compatibility mode as defined in the XSLT 3.0 specification. If a stylesheet specifies version="1.0", this causes certain constructs to behave in a way that retains the XSLT 1.0 behavior. XSLT 3.0 Dynamic Evaluation: Provides use of the XSLT 3.0 instruction xsl:evaluate, which allows dynamic evaluation of XPath expressions. XSLT 3.0 XPath 3.1 Feature: Provides full use of XPath 3.1 features, including XPath 3.1 functions, and maps and arrays. Note: Saxon supports XPath 3.1 unconditionally in XSLT 3.0 stylesheets, it does not offer a processing mode in which XPath is restricted to version 3.0. XSLT 3.0 Higher-Order Functions: Provides higher-order functions: specifically, the ability to use functions as values, including dynamic function calls, inline functions, partial function application, and the standard higher-order functions defined in the XPath 3.1 function library.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to work on. Can be empty if no input is needed.

parameters.name: StyleSheet
parameters.description: The stylesheet to apply.

functions.name: SQL.ClientVersion
functions.description: Gets the DBMS client API version number. The higher word contains the major client version (the XX value in the XX.YY version number); the lower word contains the minor client version (the YY value in the XX.YY version number). If an DBMS client was not set calling ClientVersion method return an error.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.result: Returns client version number or error.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.Commit
functions.description: Saves any changes and ends the current transaction. Use Commit method to write transaction changes permanently to a database. It commits the work of all commands that associated with that connection. All changes to the database since the last commit are made permanent and cannot be undone. Before a commit, all changes made since the start of the transaction can be rolled back using SQL.Rollback method.

parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.Connect
functions.signature: MBS( "SQL.Connect"; Connection; DBString { ; UserName; Password; Client } )
functions.description: Opens the connection to a data source. Be aware that for most database servers, you need to specify client library with SQL.SetConnectionOption . For server scripting, please use one connection for each script running on server, so multiple scripts running at the same data don't access the same connection or command objects in memory. And each connection has its own transaction and error state. see also https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_connection.html If you get a crash here on MacOS, please check if crash report says Code Signature Invalid. Then you may need to code sign the dylib yourself to match the code signature of your runtime application. Connect to Microsoft Access, FileMaker Server (or Pro), Microsoft Visual FoxPro and others via ODBC. For SQLite, you can use SQL.SQLite3.SetKey to set encryption key to use for encrypted databases. Use SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.Activate to activate the internal SQLite library. We have a collection of library files here: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/files/Libs/ If you have trouble with DLLs for databases not loading on Windows, please check if you have new Visual Studio Runtime libraries installed. If you need more than one DLL, please use Process.SetDllDirectory to point to the folder to find the DLL files. It can help to put the DLL in the same folder as the FileMaker (server) application.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: DBString
parameters.description: Database connection string. Please specify port with comma after IP, not double colon.

parameters.name: UserName
parameters.description: The user name to use when establishing the connection.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password to use when establishing the connection.

parameters.name: Client
parameters.description: Optional, one of the following client values: CubeSQL, SQLAnywhere, ODBC, Oracle, SQLServer, Firebird, InterBase, SQLBase, DB2, DuckDB, Informix, Sybase, MySQL, PostgreSQL or SQLite.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: MySQL: Connect and run a query:
examples.text_code:
#Start a new connection
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
")]
#Tell plugin where MySQL library is (put it where you like)
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
; "MYSQL.LIBS"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/libmysqlclient.dylib")]
#Connect to a mysql database:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.Connect";
$Connection
; "
192.168
.
11.51
@Server_Config"; "user"; "password"; "MySQL")]
If [
$result
≠  "OK"]
#Connection failed
Show Custom Dialog ["Error: " &
$result
]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
)]
Halt Script
Else
#Create a query:
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "SELECT * FROM Server_Config where ServerName=:Name")]
#If you use parameters, you can fill them here
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsText
";
$Command
; "Name"; "MacMini")]
#Execute it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
)]
If [
$result
≠ "OK"]
Set Field [
MySQL Query::Result
;
$result
]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Set Variable [
$lines
; Value:""]
Set Variable [
$fieldcount
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FieldCount
";
$command
)]
Loop
#call FetchNext to get the next record
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
)]
Exit Loop If [
$result
≠ 1]
Set Variable [
$line
; Value:""]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:1]
Loop
#We query field names and values to show them later
Set Variable [
$v
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$command
;
$i
)]
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldName
";
$command
;
$i
)]
Set Variable [
$line
; Value:
$line
&
$n
& ": " &
$v
& ¶]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+1]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>
$fieldCount
]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$lines
; Value:
$lines
& (
$line
& ¶)]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$lines

$lines
& ¶]
Show Custom Dialog ["Result from Query:";
$lines
]

";

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Start a new connection</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Tell plugin where MySQL library is (put it where you like)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $Connection; "MYSQL.LIBS"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/libmysqlclient.dylib")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Connect to a mysql database:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Connect"; $Connection; "192.168.11.51@Server_Config"; "user"; "password"; "MySQL")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result  ≠  "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Connection failed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error: " & $result</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="90" name="Halt Script"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Create a query:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; "SELECT * FROM Server_Config where ServerName=:Name")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Command</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>If you use parameters, you can fill them here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetParamAsText"; $Command; "Name"; "MacMini")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Execute it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result  ≠ "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result</Calculation>
    <Field table="MySQL Query" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error: " & $result</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lines</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FieldCount"; $command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fieldcount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>call FetchNext to get the next record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$result  ≠ 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$line</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>We query field names and values to show them later</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsText"; $command; $i)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$v</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldName"; $command; $i)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$line &  $n & ": " & $v & ¶</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$line</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i+1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$i > $fieldCount</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$lines & ($line & ¶)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lines</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$lines & ¶</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lines</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Result from Query:"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$lines</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result2</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: MySQL: Run SSH Tunnel to connect to MySQL:
examples.text_code:
# some settings like local IP/Port
Set Variable [
$LocalIP
; Value: "
127.0
.
0.1
" ]
Set Variable [
$LocalPort
; Value: 3306 ]
# the SSH server to connect through
Set Variable [
$SSHServerIP
; Value: "myserver.test" ]
# database to connect
Set Variable [
$DatabaseIP
; Value: "db.myserver.test" ]
Set Variable [
$DatabasePort
; Value: 3306 ]
# create connection
Set Variable [
$ssh
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Connect
";
$ssh
;
$SSHServerIP
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.SessionHandshake
";
$ssh
) ]
# using private key file here, but username+password is also possible
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthPublicKeyFile
";
$ssh
; "username"; "" ;
$keypair_path
; "password" ) ]
Set Variable [
$auth
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.IsAuthenticated
";
$ssh
) ]
# must be 1
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Run
";
$ssh
;
$DatabaseIP
;
$DatabasePort
;
$LocalIP
;
$LocalPort
) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .5 ]
Set Variable [
$tunnel_mess
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Messages
";
$ssh
) ]
# now this should show that tunnel is waiting.
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
; "MYSQL.LIBS";
$mysql_path
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetClient
";
$Connection
; "MySQL") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.Connect";
$Connection
;
$LocalIP
& "," &
$LocalPort
& "@DatabaseName"; "UserName"; "password"; "MySQL") ]
Set Variable [
$tunnel_mess
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Messages
";
$ssh
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$tunnel_mess
&  ¶ &
$r
]
# should show OK for connect and for tunnel that it's forwarding data
# do query here
# disconnect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Cancel
";
$ssh
) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Disconnect
";
$ssh
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Release
";
$ssh
) ]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> some settings like local IP/Port</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"127.0.0.1" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LocalIP</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>3306 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LocalPort</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> the SSH server to connect through</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"myserver.test" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SSHServerIP</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>3306 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DatabasePort</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create connection</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.SessionHandshake"; $ssh) </Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$auth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> must be 1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Tunnel.Run"; $ssh; $DatabaseIP; $DatabasePort; $LocalIP; $LocalPort) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>.5 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Tunnel.Messages"; $ssh ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$tunnel_mess</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now this should show that tunnel is waiting.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $Connection; "MYSQL.LIBS"; $mysql_path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetClient"; $Connection; "MySQL") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Connect"; $Connection; $LocalIP & "," & $LocalPort & "@DatabaseName"; "UserName"; "password"; "MySQL") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Tunnel.Messages"; $ssh ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$tunnel_mess</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $tunnel_mess &  ¶ & $r </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> should show OK for connect and for tunnel that it's forwarding data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do query here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> disconnect</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Tunnel.Cancel"; $ssh ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>.1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Disconnect"; $ssh ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SSH.Release"; $ssh ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:

("
SQL.NewConnection
") ]
# Tell plugin where PostgreSQL library is
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Library/Application Support/FileMaker/Extensions/libpq
.5
.dylib" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
; "LIBPQ.LIBS";
$path
) ]
# you can include an app name to show in server administration views
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
; "APPNAME"; "FileMaker") ]
# Connection string in the following format:
# "" or "@" - empty string or '@' character, connects to a local server
# database_name or @database_name - connects to a database with the specified name on local server
# server_name@ - connects to the specified server
# server_name@database_name - connects to a database with the specified name on the specified server
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.Connect";
$Connection
; "server@database"; "user"; "password"; "PostgreSQL") ]
If [
$result
≠  "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]
Halt Script
End If

functions.name: SQL.ConvertDateTimeFromSQL
functions.description: Converts a date or timestamp to FileMaker format. Localization of FileMaker time and date formats applies. Examples below are in German format. This function doesn't do time zones. If you use SQL record sets, you don't need this function, but SQL.GetFieldAsTime , SQL.GetFieldAsDateTime , SQL.GetFieldAsDate , SQL.GetParamAsTime , SQL.GetParamAsDateTime and SQL.GetParamAsDate do similar conversion for you internally.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The text value.

functions.result: Returns date, time, timestamp or error.

examples.label: Convert time stamp:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.ConvertDateTimeToSQL
functions.description: Converts a date/time/timestamp to SQL text. Localization of FileMaker time and date formats applies. Examples below are in German format. Similar function is used internally by SQL.SetParamValue , SQL.SetParamAsDate , SQL.SetParamAsDateTime and SQL.SetParamAsTime to convert values automatically for you.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The date, time or timestamp value. Text or number are converted by FileMaker to get a timestamp.

functions.name: SQL.Disconnect
functions.description: Disconnects the connection from the database.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.Execute
functions.description: Executes the current command. Use the Execute method to execute the query or stored procedure specified in the command text. Execute method calls Prepare method implicitly if needed. If the command has input parameters, they should be bound before calling Execute method. Input parameters represented by param object. To bind input variables, use the SetParam commands. A command (an SQL statement or procedure) can have a result set after executing. To check whether a result set exists use SQL.isResultSet method. If result set exists, a set of Field objects is created after command execution. Rows from the result set can be fetched one by one using SQL.FetchNext method. To get field description or value use Field method. Output parameters represented by param objects. They are available after command execution.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

examples.label: Executes a command:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

; Value:

SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "CREATE TABLE Test(FirstName VARCHAR, LastName VARCHAR, Birthday date, NumberOfOrders integer, TotalSales double)") ]
# run it

$Command

SQL.Commit
";
$Connection
) ]

Set Variable [
$result4
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
) ]

" ;

; "connection string" ; "user" ; "password" ; "ODBC" ) ]

) ]

$StoredProcedure

examples.label: Run geopoly function to create svg:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
command =
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
"; 22001; "SELECT '<svg version=\"
1.1
\" viewBox=\"0 0 600 300\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" xmlns:xlink=\"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink\">';
WITH t1(x,y,n,color) AS (VALUES
(100,100,3,'red'),
(200,100,4,'orange'),
(300,100,5,'green'),
(400,100,6,'blue'),
(500,100,7,'purple'),
(100,200,8,'red'),
(200,200,10,'orange'),
(300,200,12,'green'),
(400,200,16,'blue'),
(500,200,20,'purple')
)
SELECT
geopoly_svg(geopoly_regular(x,y,40,n),
printf('style=\"fill:none;stroke:%s;stroke-width:2\"',color))
|| printf(' <text x=\"%d\" y=\"%d\" alignment-baseline=\"central\" text-anchor=\"middle\">%d</text>',x,y+6,n)
FROM t1;
SELECT '</svg>';");
status =
MBS
("SQL.Execute"; command);
result =
MBS
("
SQL.GetRecordsAsText
"; command) &
MBS
("
SQL.GetRecordsAsText
"; command) &
MBS
("
SQL.GetRecordsAsText
"; command);
freed =
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
"; Command)
]; result)
// adapted from https://sqlite.org/geopoly.html
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
command = MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; 22001; "SELECT '<svg version=\"1.1\" viewBox=\"0 0 600 300\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" xmlns:xlink=\"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink\">';
WITH t1(x,y,n,color) AS (VALUES
  (100,100,3,'red'),
  (200,100,4,'orange'),
  (300,100,5,'green'),
  (400,100,6,'blue'),
  (500,100,7,'purple'),
  (100,200,8,'red'),
  (200,200,10,'orange'),
  (300,200,12,'green'),
  (400,200,16,'blue'),
  (500,200,20,'purple')
)
SELECT
  geopoly_svg(geopoly_regular(x,y,40,n),
       printf('style=\"fill:none;stroke:%s;stroke-width:2\"',color))
  || printf(' <text x=\"%d\" y=\"%d\" alignment-baseline=\"central\" text-anchor=\"middle\">%d</text>',x,y+6,n)
 FROM t1;
SELECT '</svg>';");
status = MBS("SQL.Execute"; command);
result = MBS("SQL.GetRecordsAsText"; command) & MBS("SQL.GetRecordsAsText"; command) & MBS("SQL.GetRecordsAsText"; command);
freed = MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; Command)
]; result)

// adapted from https://sqlite.org/geopoly.html</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.FetchFirst
functions.description: Fetches the first row from a result set. Use SQL.FetchPrior , SQL.FetchFirst, SQL.FetchLast method to fetch rows from the scrollable result set. The scrollable result set option should be set before Command executed (SQLite and InterBase don't support this feature). See server specific notes to discover how SQLAPI supports the scrollable result set for certain DBMS. To check whether a result set exists use SQL.isResultSet method. Scrolling only works if enabled, so please call SQL.SetCommandOption function to enable it if needed. Result is 1 on success or 0 on failure.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

examples.label: Fetch first record:
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchFirst"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.FetchNext
functions.description: Fetches next row from a result set. Use FetchNext method to fetch row by row from the result set. You need to call it a first time to get to first row after you made a query. To check whether a result set exists use SQL.isResultSet method. Result is 1 on success or 0 on failure.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run command and fetch first record
examples.text_code:
# create command
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "SELECT * FROM Test")]
# run select
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
)]
# go to first row
Set Variable [
$result3
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.FetchNext";
$Command
)]
#
$result3
is 1 if we got a record
# read first name field from result:
Set Variable [
$firstname
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$command
; "FirstName")]
# later release command
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
)]

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $Command)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

functions.name: SQL.FetchPos
functions.description: Fetches a row from a result set. You may need to request recordset to be scrollable to have this work. For that, please call MBS( " SQL.SetCommandOption "; $Command; "Scrollable"; "True" ) before doing the query. Result is 1 on success or 0 on failure.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: The index/offset of the row. Zero based.

parameters.name: Relative
parameters.description: Pass 1 for relative movement.

Scrolling only works if enabled, so please call SQL.SetCommandOption function to enable it if needed. Result is 1 on success or 0 on failure.
