functions.description: Returns the number of fields (columns) in a result set. FieldCount method returns the number of fields created implicitly after the command execution if a result set exists.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.result: Returns field count or error message.

functions.name: SQL.FieldExists
functions.description: Checks whether a field exists.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name of the field to check.

functions.name: SQL.FreeAllConnections
functions.description: Frees all SQL connections and commands.

functions.name: SQL.FreeCommand
functions.description: Frees the command and releases all memory associated with it.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

examples.label: Free command object:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.FreeCommand"; $Command )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.FreeConnection
functions.description: Call this method if you are done with a connection. This closes the connection and frees all memory allocated for it. Internally calls SQL.Disconnect if needed.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

examples.label: Free connection object:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetAutoCommit
functions.description: Checks whether autocommit is enabled or disabled for the current connection. Function returns autocommit option state that was previously set by SQL.SetAutoCommit . Returns "Unknown" if no autocommit mode was set. In that case see DBMS specific documentation for default autocommit option used.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.GetCommandOption
functions.description: Gets a string value of a specific command option. Call this method to get value(s) of server specific command options. See Server specific notes. A command related option may be specified in either connection object or SACommand object. If it is specified in connection object it affects all the commands on that connection. If an option is not specially assigned to the command object the Library takes this option from related connection object (see SQL.GetConnectionOption method). If an option is not specified in neither command nor connection objects, the Library takes the default option value. see also https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_command.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the command option.

functions.name: SQL.GetCommandOptions
functions.description: Queries list of options for command.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.GetCommandTag
functions.description: Queries tag for command.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.GetConnectionOption
functions.description: Gets a string value of a specific connection option. Call this method to get value(s) of server specific connection or command options. See Server specific notes. If an option is not specified in connection objects, the Library takes the default option value. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_connection.html

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the connection or command option.

functions.result: Returns the value or error message.

functions.name: SQL.GetConnectionOptions
functions.description: Queries list of options for connection.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.GetConnectionTag
functions.description: Queries tag for connection.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsBoolean
functions.description: Returns field's value as boolean value; converts if needed. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_field.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

examples.label: Read null value as empty, otherwise get boolean:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If(
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldIsNull"; $command; "FieldName"); 
   "";
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsBoolean"; $command; "FieldName"))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsContainer
functions.description: Queries field value as container. If you can provide a file name, we can use it to auto detect the type of data and import it in the right format, e.g. add a preview for PDF or the image size for JPEG.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename for the container. This will determinate the type of data to FileMaker.

examples.label: Query image field value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.GetFieldAsContainer"; $Command; "MyBlob"; $FileName )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract BLOBs from a Oracle database and store in files:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
;"OCI8.LIBS";"/Users/test/Downloads/instantclient_19_8/libclntsh.dylib") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Connect
";
$Connection
;"(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=TCP)(HOST=
10.128
.
12.150
)(PORT=1521))(CONNECT_DATA=(SID=STRMTL)))";"User”;"Password";"Oracle") ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to connect";
$result
]
Else
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value: "SELECT FILE_NAME, FILE_DATA, FILE_DESCRIPTION FROM UPLOADED_FILE OFFSET 1452000 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY" ]
Set Variable [
$cmd
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
;
$sql
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse SQL";
$cmd
]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$cmd
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run query";
$r
]
Else
Set Variable [
$folderPath
; Value: "/Users/test/Desktop/PDF/folder" ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$cmd
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
# Failed to get record?
Show Custom Dialog [
$r
]
Else If [
$r
= 0 ]
# no records found
Else
Loop
# get file name
Set Variable [
$filename
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$cmd
; "FILE_NAME") ]
# get as BLOB with PDF
Set Variable [
$compressed
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.GetFieldAsContainer";
$cmd
; "FILE_DATA";
$fileName
) ]
#
# store in field
// New Record/Request
// Set Field [
SQL_API::PDF
;
$PDF
]
// Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]
#
# write to file
Set Variable [
$filePath
; Value:
MBS
("
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$folderPath
;
$filename
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Container.WriteFile
";
$PDF
;
$filePath
) ]
#
# loop ends after last field
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$cmd
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
End Loop
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$cmd
) ]
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption";$Connection;"OCI8.LIBS";"/Users/test/Downloads/instantclient_19_8/libclntsh.dylib") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Connect";$Connection;"(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=TCP)(HOST=10.128.12.150)(PORT=1521))(CONNECT_DATA=(SID=STRMTL)))";"User”;"Password";"Oracle") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to connect"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>

    </Message>
    <Buttons>

        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"SELECT FILE_NAME, FILE_DATA, FILE_DESCRIPTION FROM UPLOADED_FILE OFFSET 1452000 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; $sql) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cmd</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to parse SQL"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$cmd </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run query"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/test/Desktop/PDF/folder" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folderPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Failed to get record?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $r </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get file name</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsText"; $cmd; "FILE_NAME") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get as BLOB with PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsContainer"; $cmd; "FILE_DATA"; $fileName) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$compressed</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store in field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="False" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PDF </Calculation>
    <Field table="SQL_API" id="99999" name="PDF "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write to file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; $folderPath; $filename) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.WriteFile"; $PDF; $filePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop ends after last field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsDate

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.result: Returns date value or error message.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

   MBS("SQL.GetFieldIsNull"; $command; "FieldName"); 
   "";
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsDate"; $command; "FieldName"))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsFloat

For integer values, you can use SQL.GetFieldAsNumber . see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_field.html For some databases, you get better precision, if you cast/convert to float data type in the SQL statement.

parameters.name: Field

examples.label: Read null value as empty, otherwise get number:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If(
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldIsNull"; $command; "FieldName"); 
   "";
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsNumber"; $command; "FieldName"))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsPDF
functions.description: Returns field's value as PDF value. Field must be a BLOB field. The plugin does not verify if the BLOB contains a valid PDF document. See also SQL.GetFieldAsJPEG and SQL.GetFieldAsPNG . For PDF content: If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container.

functions.result: Returns container value or error message.

examples.label: Read field value of field "PDFData" into variable:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsPDF"; $command; "PDFData")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$firstname</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsPNG
functions.description: Returns field's value as PNG value. Field must be a BLOB field. The plugin does not verify if the BLOB contains a valid PNG image. See also SQL.GetFieldAsJPEG and SQL.GetFieldAsPDF .

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.result: Returns container value or error message.

examples.label: Run a stored procedure:
examples.text_code:
# new command for calling our test procedure on a Microsoft SQL Server
Set Variable [
$command
;
MBS
( "
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "dbo.Test" ) ]
# set a parameter
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.SetParamAsNumber
";
$Command
; "Param1"; 7 ) ]
# execute query
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
# fetch first record
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
) ]
# get result field
Set Variable [
$result
;
MBS
( "SQL.GetFieldAsText";
$Command
; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> new command for calling our test procedure on a Microsoft SQL Server</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; "dbo.Test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$command</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set a parameter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SetParamAsNumber"; $Command; "Param1"; 7 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> execute query</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.Execute"; $Command ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> fetch first record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.FetchNext"; $Command ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get result field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.GetFieldAsText"; $Command; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract BLOBs from a Oracle database and store in files:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";
$Connection
;"OCI8.LIBS";"/Users/test/Downloads/instantclient_19_8/libclntsh.dylib") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Connect
";
$Connection
;"(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=TCP)(HOST=
10.128
.
12.150
)(PORT=1521))(CONNECT_DATA=(SID=STRMTL)))";"User”;"Password";"Oracle") ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to connect";
$result
]
Else
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value: "SELECT FILE_NAME, FILE_DATA, FILE_DESCRIPTION FROM UPLOADED_FILE OFFSET 1452000 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY" ]
Set Variable [
$cmd
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
;
$sql
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse SQL";
$cmd
]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$cmd
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run query";
$r
]
Else
Set Variable [
$folderPath
; Value: "/Users/test/Desktop/PDF/folder" ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$cmd
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
# Failed to get record?
Show Custom Dialog [
$r
]
Else If [
$r
= 0 ]
# no records found
Else
Loop
# get file name
Set Variable [
$filename
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.GetFieldAsText";
$cmd
; "FILE_NAME") ]
# get as BLOB with PDF
Set Variable [
$compressed
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldAsContainer
";
$cmd
; "FILE_DATA";
$fileName
) ]
#
# store in field
// New Record/Request
// Set Field [
SQL_API::PDF
;
$PDF
]
// Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]
#
# write to file
Set Variable [
$filePath
; Value:
MBS
("
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$folderPath
;
$filename
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Container.WriteFile
";
$PDF
;
$filePath
) ]
#
# loop ends after last field
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$cmd
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
End Loop
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$cmd
) ]
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption";$Connection;"OCI8.LIBS";"/Users/test/Downloads/instantclient_19_8/libclntsh.dylib") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Connect";$Connection;"(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=TCP)(HOST=10.128.12.150)(PORT=1521))(CONNECT_DATA=(SID=STRMTL)))";"User”;"Password";"Oracle") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to connect"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"SELECT FILE_NAME, FILE_DATA, FILE_DESCRIPTION FROM UPLOADED_FILE OFFSET 1452000 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; $sql) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cmd</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to parse SQL"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$cmd </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run query"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/test/Desktop/PDF/folder" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folderPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Failed to get record?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $r </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> no records found</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get file name</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsText"; $cmd; "FILE_NAME") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get as BLOB with PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsContainer"; $cmd; "FILE_DATA"; $fileName) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$compressed</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store in field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="False" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PDF </Calculation>
    <Field table="SQL_API" id="99999" name="PDF "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write to file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; $folderPath; $filename) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.WriteFile"; $PDF; $filePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop ends after last field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; $cmd) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldAsTime
functions.description: Returns field's value as time value; converts if needed. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_field.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldDataType
functions.description: Returns field data type. The field data type can be: Bool, Short, UShort, Long, ULong, Double, Numeric, DateTime, Interval, String, Bytes, LongBinary, LongChar, BLob, CLob, Cursor, SpecificToDBMS or unknown

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.result: The field type or error message.

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldIsNull
functions.description: Whether the field value is NULL.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.result: Returns 1 if the value of current object is NULL; otherwise 0.

examples.label: Read null value as empty, otherwise get floating number:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>

   MBS("SQL.GetFieldIsNull"; $command; "FieldName"); 
   "";
   MBS("SQL.GetFieldAsFloat"; $command; "FieldName"))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create a command, fill parameters and query names and values list:
examples.text_code:
#Create select
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "SELECT * FROM Test")]
#Run it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
)]
#get first row of result
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
)]
# Now query field names

$FieldNames
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.GetFieldNameList";
$Command
)]
# and field values of first row
Set Variable [
$FieldValues
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldValueList
";
$Command
)]

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldOption
functions.description: Gets a string value of a specific field option. Call this method to get value(s) of server specific field options. See Server specific notes. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_field.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the command option.

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldOptions
functions.description: Queries list of options for field.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldValue
functions.description: Returns field's value. The value is converted to a FileMaker value with matching type. BLOBs are returned as hex strings and not as container values (could be changed). NULL values are returned as empty container values.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldValueList
functions.description: Queries list of field values. If value contains a new line or return character, we replace it with \n or \r.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

examples.label: Create a command, fill parameters and query names and values list:
examples.text_code:
#Create select
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "SELECT * FROM Test")]
#Run it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
)]
#get first row of result
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
)]
# Now query field names

$FieldNames
; Value:

("
SQL.GetFieldNameList
";
$Command
)]
# and field values of first row
Set Variable [
$FieldValues
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.GetFieldValueList";
$Command
)]

functions.name: SQL.GetFieldsAsJSON
functions.description: Queries fields values current record as JSON object. Dates, Intervals and BLOBs get converted to text. Boolean, Integer, Numeric and Double types are converted to numbers in JSON. Boolean is passed through as boolean. Null or unknown field types are returned as NULL in JSON.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

examples.label: Show records as JSON:
examples.text_code:
# Create select and run it
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "SELECT * FROM Test") ]
# Run it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
If [
$result
≠ "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Loop
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$result
≠ 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "JSON" ;
MBS
( "SQL.GetFieldsAsJSON";
$Command
) ]
End Loop
End If
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Create select and run it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; "SELECT * FROM Test") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Command</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Run it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $result  ≠ "OK" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error: " & $result </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FetchNext"; $Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $result  ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "JSON" ; MBS( "SQL.GetFieldsAsJSON"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$Command ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; $Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result2</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.GetIsolationLevel
functions.description: Gets the transaction isolation level. Function returns isolation level that was previously set by SQL.SetIsolationLevel . Returns "Unknown" if no isolation level was set. In that case see DBMS specific documentation for default isolation level used. Returns one of the following strings: ReadUncommitted, ReadCommitted, RepeatableRead, Serializable or Unknown.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.result: Returns level name or error message.

parameters.name: Param
parameters.description: The parameter name or ID. Pass number for ID, a text with parameter name or the index with # prefix. e.g. "#0" for first parameter.

functions.result: Returns the value or error message.

For PDF content: If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container.

functions.result: Returns container value or error message.

functions.name: SQL.GetParamNameList

parameters.name: Command

examples.label: Create a command, fill parameters and query names and values list:
examples.text_code:
# Create command for insert
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "INSERT INTO 'Test' (FirstName, LastName, Birthday, NumberOfOrders, TotalSales) VALUES (:1,:2,:3,:4,:5)")]
# now fill some parameters
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsText
";
$Command
; 1;
SQLite fun::FirstName
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsText
";
$Command
; 2;
SQLite fun::LastName
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsDate
";
$Command
; 3;
SQLite fun::Birthday
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsNumber
";
$Command
; 4;
SQLite fun::NumberOfOrders
)]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetParamAsFloat
";
$Command
; 5;
SQLite fun::TotalSales
)]

functions.name: SQL.GetParamsAsJSON
functions.description: Queries parameters as JSON object. Great function for debugging to check if parameters are set correctly. Dates, Intervals and BLOBs get converted to text. Boolean, Integer, Numeric and Double types are converted to numbers in JSON. Boolean is passed through as boolean. Null or unknown field types are returned as NULL in JSON.

functions.name: SQL.GetRecordsAsText
functions.description: Queries all records as text. Don't call SQL.FetchNext before as this function will call it for you to loop over records. Converts all field content to text. If you just want one field, maybe you just select the required field with your select statement. Warning: If the text contains the delimiters for fields and rows, you may get strange results. You can of course substitute these in the SQL query. See the REPLACE() function in SQL.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: FieldDelimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter for between fields. Default is a tab character.

parameters.name: RowDelimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter between records. Default is newline character.

functions.name: SQL.InsertOrUpdateRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "SQL.InsertOrUpdateRecords"; Command; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; PrimaryKeyDB1; PrimaryKeyFM1 { ; SecondaryKeyDB2; SecondaryKeyFM2; TertariyKeyDB3; TertariyKeyFM3 } )
functions.description: Creates or updates records in FileMaker database for current recordset. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from SQL database into FileMaker table. Please provide field names in FileMaker. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. This will not work for BLOBs as they are stored currently as hex encoded text only. Function will fail if data types in FileMaker and other SQL database do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. Fixed in 10.2, so it may work now. For copying record within FileMaker, please use FM.InsertRecordQuery or FM.SQL.InsertRecords functions. Returns list with three values: Number of records inserted, number of records updated and number of errors. Primary key in FM must be defined as unique and validate always, so FileMaker properly reports a duplicate. We first do a select to count how many we have and then do either insert or update. If insert returns duplicate error, we try update, too. Up to ten pairs of DB and FM keys are possible. This function requires working indexes in the table. And don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeyDB1
parameters.description: The name of the primary key in the record set. Must be one of the columns in the record from SQL. If empty, we use primaryKeyFM1 here.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeyFM1
parameters.description: The name of the primary key for the update statements. Must be one of the columns in the FileMaker table. If empty, we use primaryKeyDB1 here.

parameters.name: SecondaryKeyDB2
parameters.description: The name of the secondary key in the record set. Must be one of the columns in the record from SQL. If empty, we use primaryKeyFM2 here.

parameters.name: SecondaryKeyFM2
parameters.description: The name of the secondary key for the update statements. Must be one of the columns in the FileMaker table. If empty, we use primaryKeyDB2 here.

parameters.name: TertariyKeyDB3
parameters.description: The name of the tertary key in the record set. Must be one of the columns in the record from SQL. If empty, we use primaryKeyFM3 here.

parameters.name: TertariyKeyFM3
parameters.description: The name of the tertary key for the update statements. Must be one of the columns in the FileMaker table. If empty, we use primaryKeyDB3 here.

functions.name: SQL.InsertRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "SQL.InsertRecords"; Command; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames { ; IgnoreDuplicates } )
functions.description: Creates records in FileMaker database for current recordset. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from SQL database into FileMaker table. Please provide field names in FileMaker. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. This will not work for BLOBs as they are stored currently as hex encoded text only. Function will fail if data types in FileMaker and other SQL database do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. For copying record within FileMaker, please use FM.InsertRecordQuery or FM.SQL.InsertRecords functions. Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: IgnoreDuplicates
parameters.description: If 1, ignores duplicates or with 0 or by default reports error for duplicates. If duplicates are found, the function returns number of duplicates instead of OK.

functions.result: Returns number of duplicates, OK or error.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.Activate
functions.description: Activates the internal SQLite library. If you use this command, you don't need to provide sqlite3.dll or sqlite3.dylib yourself. Our internal SQLite library includes the following extensions: JSON1, the extension to provide JSON functions FTS5, the full text search extension in version 5 R*Tree index extension SOUNDEX function SQL math functions Geopoly extension ICU extension for unicode handling The RBU Extension SQLite Encryption Extension You can turn on these SQLite extensions at runtime with MBS Plugin: UUID extension Base64 extension CSV extension More extensions could be added on request.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.Deactivate
functions.description: Deactivates the use of internal SQLite library. You should normally never need to call this.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.Dump
functions.description: Convert an SQLite database into SQL statements that will recreate that database. This subroutine converts the content of an SQLite database into UTF-8 text SQL statements that can be used to exactly recreate the original database.  ROWID values are preserved. The connection handle parameter is the SQLite database connection using our internal SQLite library. Schema is the schema within that database which is to be dumped.  Usually the zSchema is "main" but can also be "temp" or any ATTACH-ed database.  If Table is not empty, then only the content of that one table is dumped.  If zTable is empty, then all tables are dumped. The generate text is collected and returned as a list.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Schema
parameters.description: Which schema to dump.  Usually "main".

parameters.name: Table
parameters.description: Which table to dump. “” means everything.

$Connection
; "main" ) ]

$r
; Value:

SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]

functions.description: Queries whether the CSV extensions is enabled.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.GetUUIDExtensionEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether the uuid extensions is enabled.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.ICUEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether ICU libraries are enabled. If MBS Plugin can find International Components for Unicode library files, we can load them and use them for proper unicode handling in SQLite. Default is 1 to enable them, if possible.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.ICULoaded
functions.description: Queries whether ICU libraries are loaded. If MBS Plugin can find International Components for Unicode library files, we can load them and use them for proper unicode handling in SQLite.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.ICUUsed
functions.description: Queries whether ICU libraries have been used. Once SQLite is initialized, we set this to 1 if we passed the functionality to SQLite.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.LoadICU
functions.description: Loads ICU libraries now. Returns 1 on success and 0 on failure. Normally we load them when SQLite initializes and we then add the ICU extension if we find the library. Call this function toe explicit load them now. If MBS Plugin can find International Components for Unicode library files, we can load them and use them for proper unicode handling in SQLite.

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.SetBase64ExtensionEnabled
functions.description: Whether to enable the Base64 extensions. This is a SQLite extension for converting in either direction between a (binary) blob and base64 text. Base64 can transit a sane USASCII channel unmolested. It also plays nicely in CSV or written as TCL brace-enclosed literals or SQL string literals, and can be used unmodified in XML-like documents. This is an independent implementation of conversions specified in RFC 4648, done on the above date by the author (Larry Brasfield) who thereby has the right to put this into the public domain. The conversions meet RFC 4648 requirements, provided that this C source specifies that line-feeds are included in the encoded data to limit visible line lengths to 72 characters and to terminate any encoded blob having non-zero length. Length limitations are not imposed except that the runtime SQLite string or blob length limits are respected. Otherwise, any length binary sequence can be represented and recovered. Generated base64 sequences, with their line-feeds included, can be concatenated; the result converted back to binary will be the concatenation of the represented binary sequences. This SQLite3 extension creates a function, base64(x), which either: converts text x containing base64 to a returned blob; or converts a blob x to returned text containing base64. An error will be thrown for other input argument types.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Enable feature:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.SetBase64ExtensionEnabled"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.SetCSVExtensionEnabled
functions.description: Whether to enable the CSV extensions. This file contains the implementation of an SQLite virtual table for reading CSV files. Usage: .load ./csv CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE temp.csv USING csv(filename=FILENAME); SELECT * FROM csv; The columns are named "c1", "c2", "c3", ... by default.  Or the application can define its own CREATE TABLE statement using the schema= parameter, like this: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE temp.csv2 USING csv( filename = "../http.log", schema = "CREATE TABLE x(date,ipaddr,url,referrer,userAgent)" ); Instead of specifying a file, the text of the CSV can be loaded using the data= parameter. If the columns=N parameter is supplied, then the CSV file is assumed to have N columns.  If both the columns= and schema= parameters are omitted, then the number and names of the columns is determined by the first line of the CSV input.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: SQL.MySQL.InsertID
functions.description: For MySQL Connection queries the last insert ID. Please query value right after doing Insert. This value is reset when you call commit. see also http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.1/en/mysql-insert-id.html

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.result: Returns the ID or error message.

functions.name: SQL.NewCommand
functions.description: Creates a new command. Don't forget to free command with SQL.FreeCommand . SQL command reference numbers are starting at 23000 and counting up for each new command. For server scripting, please use one connection for each script running on server, so multiple scripts running at the same data don't access the same connection or command objects in memory. And each connection has its own transaction and error state.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: This represents command text string (an SQL statement or a stored procedure name). If it is empty string, no command text is associated with the command, and you have to call SQL.SetCommandText method later.

parameters.name: CommandType
parameters.description: Type of a command. Can be CmdSQLStmt, CmdSQLStmtRaw or CmdStoredProc.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: The ID to use instead of default one. Must be unique. If none is provided, the plugin will create one.

functions.result: Returns command reference number or error message.

examples.label: Run a stored procedure:

;

( "SQL.NewCommand";
$Connection
; "dbo.Test" ) ]
# set a parameter

$r
;

( "
SQL.SetParamAsNumber
";

; "Param1"; 7 ) ]
# execute query

$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
# fetch first record
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
) ]
# get result field
Set Variable [
$result
;
MBS
( "
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$Command
; 1 ) ]

examples.label: Call stored procedure sys.sp_setapprole via ODBC connection to Microsoft SQL Server:
examples.text_code:
# connect via SQL to a Microsoft SQL Server
Set Variable [
$$Connection
; Value:

SQL.NewConnection

; "ODBC" ) ]

If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]

$StoredProcedure
; Value:

( "SQL.NewCommand" ;
$$Connection
; "sys.sp_setapprole" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
# for debugging query list of parameters

; Value:

";
$StoredProcedure

Set Variable [

$StoredProcedure

$StoredProcedure

$StoredProcedure

Set Variable [

SQL.Execute
" ;
$StoredProcedure
) ]
// #
If [

("IsError") = 0 ]
# query results on success
Set Variable [

; Value:

SQL.GetParamAsNumber
" ;

$StoredProcedure

SQL.FreeCommand

$Connection
; Value:

("
SQL.NewConnection

("
SQL.SetConnectionOption
";

; "DUCKDB.LIBS"; "/opt/homebrew/Cellar/duckdb/
1.2
.1/lib/libduckdb.dylib") ]
# Connect to database

$result
; Value:

("
SQL.Connect
";
$Connection
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.duckdb"; ""; ""; "DuckDB") ]
If [
$result
≠  "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]
Else
# Create select and run it
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.NewCommand";
$Connection
; "SELECT version() AS version;") ]
# Run it
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
If [
$result
≠ "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: ";
$result
]
Else
# get first record
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
) ]
# get version value
Set Variable [
$v
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$command
; 1) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Version" ;
$v
]
End If
End If
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
) ]
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]

functions.name: SQL.NewConnection
functions.description: Initialize a new SAConnection object and returns Connection Reference. The Connection represents a database connection. You must have a connection before you can operate on the database - furthermore the connection needs to be connected. It is important to note that connection objects are not connected during construction - it is up to the user to explicitly call Connect method for the object after it is created and before it is used. Once connected, a connection object is normally shared by the other objects that require a connection (e.g. commands). Use SQL.FreeConnection to release connection after you finished. read also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_connection.html SQL connection reference numbers are starting at 22000 and counting up for each new connection.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: The ID to use instead of default one. Must be unique. If none is provided, the plugin will create one.

functions.result: An error or a connection reference number.

examples.label: Create new connection
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.Open
functions.description: Opens the specified command object. Use the Open method to open the command explicitly. A command will be implicitly opened by any method that needs an open command, therefore you don't have to call it explicitly. To test whether a command is opened use isOpened method.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.ParamCount
functions.description: Returns the number of parameters associated with the command object.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.result: Returns number of Parameters or error message.

functions.name: SQL.Prepare
functions.description: Validates and prepares the current command for execution. Prepare method compiles the command, but does not execute it. The method detects syntax errors in command text and verifies the existence of database objects. Execute method calls Prepare method implicitly if needed, therefore you don't have to call it explicitly.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.ResetCommand
functions.description: Clears all DBMS related handles and sturctures but doesn't destroy them (mostly for Unix fork/exec based applications)

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.ResetConnection
functions.description: Clears all DBMS related handles and structures but doesn't destroy them.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.Rollback
functions.description: Cancels any changes made during the current transaction and ends the transaction. Rollback method rolls back the database to the state it was in at the completion of the last commit operation. All uncommitted work is undone. Rollback method rolls back the work of all commands that associated with that connection. To commit all changes made since the start of the transaction use SQL.Commit method.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.RowsAffected
functions.description: Queries the number of rows affected. Returns the number of rows affected by the last insert/update/delete command execution. This normally only works if enabled, so please call SQL.SetCommandOption function to enable it if needed. You may need to define cursor to be scrollable or cached in order to get number of rows in a recordset (For SELECT).

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.result: Returns number of rows or error message.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.EnableLoadExtension
functions.description: Enables extensions on SQLite 3.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.description: Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.LastInsertRowID
functions.description: For SQLite3, returns the last row ID used. see also http://www.sqlite.org/c3ref/last_insert_rowid.html

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.result: Returns the ID or error message.

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.MemoryHighwater
functions.description: Queries maximum memory usage so far. Can be reset with reset parameter.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.description: Whether to reset the high water mark. Pass 1 to reset.

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.MemoryUsed
functions.description: Queries memory in use by SQLite. This is memory allocated, but not yet freed.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.ReKey
functions.description: You can change the key on a database using the Rekey Function. An empty key decrypts the database. Rekeying requires that every page of the database file be read, decrypted, reencrypted with the new key, then written out again. Consequently, rekeying can take a long time on a larger database. Most SEE variants allow you to encrypt an existing database that was created using the public domain version of SQLite. This is not possible when using the authenticating version of the encryption extension in see-aes128-ccm. If you do encrypt a database that was created with the public domain version of SQLite, no nonce will be used and the file will be vulnerable to a chosen-plaintext attach. If you call SetKey() immediately after Open when you are first creating the database, space will be reserved in the database for a nonce and the encryption will be much stronger. If you do not want to encrypt right away, call SetKey() anyway, with an empty key, and the space for the nonce will be reserved in the database even though no encryption is done initially. A public domain version of the SQLite library can read and write an encrypted database with an empty key. You only need the encryption extension if the key is non-empty.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The new encryption key.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for the key. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Change key to AES-256:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SQLite3.ReKey"; $Connection; "aes256:" & $Key; "UTF-8" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SQLite3.SetKey
functions.description: Sets the encryption key to use. This key is applied to the database after connecting. In case of an error, the plugin raises an exception. An empty key can be used for having no encryption. The amount of key material actually used by the encryption extension depends on which variant of SEE you are using. With RC4, the first 256 byte of key are used. With the AES128, the first 16 bytes of the key are used. With AES256, the first 32 bytes of key are used. If you specify a key that is shorter than the maximum key length, then the key material is repeated as many times as necessary to complete the key. If you specify a key that is larger than the maximum key length, then the excess key material is silently ignored. The key must begin with an ASCII prefix to specify which algorithm to use. The prefix must be one of "rc4:", "aes128:", or "aes256:". The prefix is not used as part of the key sent into the encryption algorithm. So the real key should begin on the first byte after the prefix. If no prefix is given, we default to AES 128. To be compatible to Xojo (Real Studio), you can use AES128. Make sure you pass right text encoding (or none for UTF-8). e.g. using "Müller" as key in text encoding Windows ANSI will not open a database which used that key in UTF-8 encoding.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The new encryption key.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for the key. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Use an encryption key:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SQLite3.SetKey"; $Connection; "xxx" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Repetition>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.ServerVersion
functions.description: The long number of the currently connected server version. The higher word contains the major server version (the XX value in the XX.YY version number); the lower word contains the minor server version (the YY value in the XX.YY version number).

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.name: SQL.ServerVersionString
functions.description: Gets the currently connected DBMS server version string. A server version string may contain some useful information about server brand, configuration and so on. It is a good idea to display this information in all your applications.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

examples.label: Show server version for connection:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$version
; Value:
MBS
( "SQL.ServerVersionString";
$Connection
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Server Version"; Message:
$version
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.ServerVersionString"; $Connection ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$version</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Server Version"; Message: $version</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SetAutoCommit
functions.description: Enables or disables autocommit for the current connection, that is, automatic commit of every SQL command. If autocommit is on, the database is committed automatically after each SQL command. Otherwise, transaction is committed only after Commit calling.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: AutoCommit
parameters.description: Whether to turn auto commit on or off. Can be "On" or "Off".

examples.label: Turn auto commit off:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SetClient
functions.description: Assigns an DBMS client for the connection. You can set a client in Connect method, in this case you don't need to use setClient method. The main destination of this method is to connect to a server with no connection to a database (for example, to check a client availability and version). But if you are calling Connect method with no specified client you have to use setClient method before. If you are calling setClient method when the connection is set to another client the previous connection will be disconnected and new client will be set. Pass one of this values: CubeSQL, SQLAnywhere, ODBC, Oracle, SQLServer, Firebird, InterBase, SQLBase, DB2, DuckDB, Informix, Sybase, MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, OLEDB or SQLite. Connect to Microsoft Access, FileMaker Server (or Pro), Microsoft Visual FoxPro and others via ODBC. Be aware that for most database servers, you need to specify client library with SQL.SetConnectionOption first. You don't need SQL.SetClient when you pass client to SQL.Connect and you should call SQL.SetConnectionOption before those.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Client
parameters.description: The name of the database client to use.

examples.label: Sets client to be SQLite:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SetClient"; $Connection; "SQLite" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SetCommandOption
functions.description: Sets a string value of a specific command option. Call this method to set value(s) of server specific command options. See Server specific notes. A command related option may be specified in either connection object or command object. If it is specified in connection object it affects all the commands on that connection. If an option is not specially assigned to the command object the Library takes this option from related connection object (see SQL.GetCommandOption function). If an option is not specified in neither command  nor connection objects, the Library takes the default option value. SQL.SetCommandOption method is safe to use in cross-server applications. An option related to a particular server does not affect others. see also https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_command.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the command option.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value.

functions.name: SQL.SetCommandTag
functions.description: Sets the tag for the command. This is a value where you can associate any value with the command.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: SQL.SetCommandText
functions.description: Sets the command text. Use SQL.setCommandText method to set the command text. Command text can also be set in SQL.NewCommand . It's not necessary to set a command type explicitly, because it is defined automatically in terms of command text string. But if you still have any reason to do it, use one of the following values:

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: CommandText
parameters.description: A string that represents command text string (an SQL statement or a stored procedure name).

parameters.name: CommandType
parameters.description: Type of command. Can be CmdUnknown, CmdSQLStmt, CmdSQLStmtRaw or CmdStoredProc. CmdUnknown is default.

functions.name: SQL.SetConnectionOption
functions.description: Sets a string value of a specific connection or command option. Call this method to set value(s) of server specific connection or command options. See Server specific notes. SetOption method is safe to use in cross-server applications. An option related to a particular server does not affect others. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_connection.html We have a collection of library files here: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/files/Libs/

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the connection or command option.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new option value.
