      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $appPath ; "cacert.pem") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sslPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $$Connection; "MYSQL_SSL_CA"; $sslPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cipher to use</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $$Connection; "MYSQL_SSL_CIPHER"; "DHE-RSA-AES256-SHA") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> require SSL</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $$Connection; "MYSQL_OPT_SSL_MODE"; "SSL_MODE_REQUIRED") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: MySQL: Write library to disk from container and initialize:
examples.text_code:
# your license key if you have one
Set Variable [
$SerialNumber
; Value:""]
# where we store the library file
Set Variable [
$tempFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" )]
If [
MBS
( "IsMacOS" )]
# library for Mac from Container
Set Variable [
$container
; Value:
MySQL::MySQLLibMacContainer
]
Set Variable [
$filename
; Value:"libmysqlclient.dylib"]
Else If [
MBS
( "IsWindows" )]
# library for Win from Container
Set Variable [
$container
; Value:
MySQL::MySQLLibWinContainer
]
Set Variable [
$filename
; Value:"libmysqlclient.dll"]
Else
Exit Script []
End If
Set Variable [
$filepath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$tempFolder
;
$filename
)]
# write file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.WriteFile
";
$container
;
$filepath
)]
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Writing file failed";
$r
]
End If
# now initialize
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "SQL.SetConnectionOption";
$connection
; "MYSQL.LIBS";
$filepath
)]
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL initialization failed";
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your license key if you have one</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SerialNumber</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> where we store the library file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$tempFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS( "IsMacOS" )</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> library for Mac from Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MySQL::MySQLLibMacContainer</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"libmysqlclient.dylib"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS( "IsWindows" )</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> library for Win from Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MySQL::MySQLLibWinContainer</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"libmysqlclient.dll"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $tempFolder; $filename)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filepath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.WriteFile"; $container; $filepath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r ≠ "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Writing file failed"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now initialize</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SetConnectionOption"; $connection; "MYSQL.LIBS"; $filepath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r ≠ "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"SQL initialization failed"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: SQL Server: Connect to Microsoft SQL Server from Mac via FreeTDS library:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
")]
#Tell plugin we want to use Microsoft SQL Server via ODBC
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetClient
";
$Connection
; "ODBC")]
#Tell plugin where freetds library is
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/libtdsodbc.dylib"]
#Connect to database
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Connect
";
$Connection
; "DRIVER={" &
$path
& "};Server=
192.168
.
2.32
;UId=SA;PWD=test;Database=test;TDS_VERSION=
7.2
;Port=1433")]
# or TDS_VERSION=
8.0
;
If [
$result
≠  "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error: " &
$result
]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
)]
Halt Script
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewConnection")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Connection</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Tell plugin we want to use Microsoft SQL Server via ODBC</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetClient"; $Connection; "ODBC")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Tell plugin where freetds library is</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/libtdsodbc.dylib"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Connect to database</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Connect"; $Connection; "DRIVER={" & $path & "};Server=192.168.2.32;UId=SA;PWD=test;Database=test;TDS_VERSION=7.2;Port=1433")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> or TDS_VERSION=8.0;</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result  ≠  "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error: " & $result</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeConnection"; $Connection)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="90" name="Halt Script"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SetConnectionTag
functions.description: Sets the tag for the connection. This is a value where you can associate any value with the connection.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: SQL.SetFieldOption
functions.description: Sets a string value of a specific field option. Call this method to set value(s) of server specific field options. See Server specific notes. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_field.html

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The name or one based index of the field.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: A string key of the command option.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new option value.

functions.name: SQL.SetIsolationLevel
functions.description: Sets the transaction isolation level. see also: https://www.sqlapi.com/ApiDoc/class_s_a_connection.html

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: IsolationLevel
parameters.description: The new isolation level. One of the following strings: ReadUncommitted, ReadCommitted, RepeatableRead or Serializable.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Create update command to fill blob field:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; "UPDATE 'Test' SET MyBlob=:image WHERE ID=:id") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Command</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetParamAsText"; $Command; "id"; 123 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.SetParamAsContainer"; $Command; "image"; MyTable::MyImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Run it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Execute"; $Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.Commit"; $Connection) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SQL.FreeCommand"; $Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result2</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run a stored procedure:
examples.text_code:
# new command for calling our test procedure on a Microsoft SQL Server
Set Variable [
$command
;
MBS
( "
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "dbo.Test" ) ]
# set a parameter
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "SQL.SetParamAsNumber";
$Command
; "Param1"; 7 ) ]
# execute query
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
# fetch first record
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SQL.FetchNext
";
$Command
) ]
# get result field
Set Variable [
$result
;
MBS
( "
SQL.GetFieldAsText
";
$Command
; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> new command for calling our test procedure on a Microsoft SQL Server</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.NewCommand"; $Connection; "dbo.Test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$command</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set a parameter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.SetParamAsNumber"; $Command; "Param1"; 7 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> execute query</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.Execute"; $Command ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> fetch first record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.FetchNext"; $Command ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get result field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.GetFieldAsText"; $Command; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SQL.SetParamDefault
functions.description: Sets the default value for this parameter. Forces DBMS API to use the default parameter value as the input value for an input or input/output parameter in a procedure. If DBMS API does not support the concept of "default parameter values" in stored procedures, this setting will be ignored. If you set this flag for the parameter that doesn't have a default value, the effect is DBMS  defined (e.g. an error can be returned or NULL can be bound). To cancel using the default parameter value you should call any other SetParam* function to bind a parameter value. To check whether this flag is set or not use SQL.GetParamIsDefault method.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

functions.name: SQL.SetParamTypesValues
functions.description: Sets all parameters for a query in one call. Please pass a type and a value for each parameter. Please match the number of parameters in your SQL query and their order. If value is the result of FM.NULL function in this plugin, the value is passed as NULL to the database. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Type and Value parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: Type...
parameters.description: Pass one type parameter for each value. Type can be Default, Null, Unknown, Integer, Double, Text, Container, Boolean, Short, UShort, Long, ULong, Date, Time or TimeStamp.

parameters.name: Value...
parameters.description: Pass one parameter for each parameter.

examples.label: Export row
examples.text_code:

Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:

("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "INSERT INTO 'Test' (FirstName, LastName, Birthday, NumberOfOrders, TotalSales) VALUES (:1,:2,:3,:4,:5)")]
# fill all parameters
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.SetParamTypesValues";
$Command
; "Text";
SQLite fun::FirstName
; "Text";
SQLite fun::LastName
; "Date";
SQLite fun::Birthday
; "Integer";
SQLite fun::NumberOfOrders
; "Double";
SQLite fun::TotalSales
)]
# Run it

$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
)]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Commit
";
$Connection
)]
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
)]

functions.name: SQL.SetParamsWithJSON
functions.description: Sets parameters based on values in JSON. If a parameter has a known type, we convert JSON to that type: Null, Double, Integer, String, Boolean. If type of parameter is not known, we check JSON type and pass boolean, numbers or texts through. Setting BLOB may not work with this function. If JSON value is array or object, we encode as text and set field to text value. Parameters not present in JSON are skipped.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command reference number gained with SQL.NewCommand .

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON object with values for the items.

examples.label: Run insert with JSON:
examples.text_code:
# Create insert command with setting values
Set Variable [
$Command
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewCommand
";
$Connection
; "INSERT INTO 'Test' (FirstName, LastName, Birthday, NumberOfOrders, TotalSales) VALUES (:FirstName,:LastName,:Birthday,:NumberOfOrders,:TotalSales)") ]
# build some JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "{}" ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddStringToObject
";
$json
; "FirstName";
SQLite fun::FirstName
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddStringToObject
";
$json
; "LastName";
SQLite fun::LastName
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddStringToObject
";
$json
; "Birthday";
SQLite fun::Birthday
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "NumberOfOrders";
SQLite fun::NumberOfOrders
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "TotalSales";
SQLite fun::TotalSales
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.SetParamsWithJSON";
$Command
;
$json
) ]
# Run it
Show Custom Dialog [ "JSON" ;
MBS
( "
SQL.GetParamsAsJSON
";
$Command
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Execute
";
$Command
) ]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Commit
";
$Connection
) ]
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeCommand
";
$Command
) ]
If [
$result
≠ "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Record exported." ]
End If

functions.name: SQL.isAlive
functions.description: Returns the database server connection status for a particular connection object. This method uses the safe query execution for most supported DBMS-es. The query uses the well known database table or procedure (mysql_ping is used for MySQL). If the query fails the method returns false.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

functions.result: Returns 1 if the database server is active and accessible; otherwise 0.

examples.label: Check alive status:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SQL.isAlive"; $Connection )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Toolbar.Install
functions.description: Installs necessary functions to modify FileMaker's toolbar. You call this functions once at startup. You can use Toolbar.Register after or before to introduce new identifiers. Use Window.SetToolbarConfiguration to install a new configuration including your custom identifiers or use customize toolbar palette.

functions.name: Toolbar.Register
functions.signature: MBS( "Toolbar.Register"; Identifier; FileName; ScriptName; Parameter; Label; PaletteLabel; Tooltip; Image )
functions.description: Registers a new toolbar button. Please use an identifier with your own prefix/suffix to make sure you have no conflict with existing identifiers. Once your item is registered, the next time the toolbar updates itself it will use it. You can use Window.SetToolbarConfiguration or the customize palette to add your toolbar item. You need to call Toolbar.Install at least once before using this command to have it take effect. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The toolbar identifier. Must be an unique string.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the FileMaker database where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to call when toolbar item is pressed.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass to the script. This can be same as identifier. Using different parameters allows you to use one script for all toolbar items.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label, which normally appears in the toolbar and in the overflow menu.

parameters.name: PaletteLabel
parameters.description: The label that appears when it is in the customization palette. Can be same as label or different.

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: The tooltip for the label!

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image to use for the toolbar item. Pass a container value with JPEG, TIFF, PNG or other image formats.

examples.label: Register custom button:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
Toolbar.Install
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "Toolbar.Register"; "TestItem"; Get(FileName); "ToolbarScript"; ""; "
MBS
Button"; "
MBS
Button"; "Just a test button";
Toolbar::Image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Toolbar.Install" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Toolbar.Register"; "TestItem"; Get(FileName); "ToolbarScript"; ""; "MBS Button"; "MBS Button"; "Just a test button"; Toolbar::Image ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.AddButton
functions.signature: MBS( "TouchBar.AddButton"; TouchBarRef; Identifier; Text; Image; CustomizationLabel; visibilityPriority { ; FileName; ScriptName; Expression } )
functions.description: Adds a simple button.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the new item.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text of the button. Can be empty to show no text.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image of the button. Pass container here with PNG for best result. JPEG may not work due to missing transparency. Can be one of the following texts to pick a default image: NSImageNameTouchBarAddDetailTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAddTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAlarmTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioInputMuteTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioInputTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioOutputMuteTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioOutputVolumeHighTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioOutputVolumeLowTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioOutputVolumeMediumTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarAudioOutputVolumeOffTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarBookmarksTemplate, NSImageNameTouchBarColorPickerFill, NSImageNameTouchBarColorPickerFont, NSImageNameTouchBarColorPickerStroke, NSImageNameTouchBarCommunicationAudioTemplate, parameters.name: CustomizationLabel
parameters.description: The customization label.

parameters.name: visibilityPriority
parameters.description: The visibility priority. Ranges from -1000 for low to 1000 for high.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name where the script is stored.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name (or ID) to trigger when button is used. Parameter to the script is the identifier.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: An expression to execute when button is pressed.

functions.name: TouchBar.AssignToApp
functions.description: Assigns the TouchBar to be the main one for current app.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

examples.label: Build App TouchBar:
examples.text_code:
#Create a new TouchBar
Set Variable [
$t
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.Create
" )]
#Add two buttons
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.AddButton
";
$t
; "AddRecord"; ""; "NSImageNameTouchBarAddTemplate"; "Add"; 0; Get(FileName); "AddRecordTouchBar" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.AddButton
";
$t
; "DeleteRecord"; ""; "NSImageNameTouchBarDeleteTemplate"; "Delete"; 0; Get(FileName); "DeleteRecordTouchBar" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.AddButton
";
$t
; "InfoButton"; "";
TouchBar::MBS
; "Info"; 0; Get(FileName); "InfoTouchBar" )]
#Set which button show by default:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.SetDefaultItemIdentifiers
";
$t
; "AddRecord¶InfoButton¶NSTouchBarItemIdentifierOtherItemsProxy")]
#Set the primary button:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.SetPrincipalItemIdentifier
";
$t
; "AddRecord" )]
#Set which buttons can be configured:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
TouchBar.SetCustomizationAllowedItemIdentifiers
";
$t
; "AddRecord¶InfoButton¶DeleteRecord")]
#Set this touch bar to be the app one:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "TouchBar.AssignToApp";
$t
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Create a new TouchBar</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.Create" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$t</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Add two buttons</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.AddButton"; $t; "AddRecord"; ""; "NSImageNameTouchBarAddTemplate"; "Add"; 0; Get(FileName); "AddRecordTouchBar" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.AddButton"; $t; "DeleteRecord"; ""; "NSImageNameTouchBarDeleteTemplate"; "Delete"; 0; Get(FileName); "DeleteRecordTouchBar" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.AddButton"; $t; "InfoButton"; ""; TouchBar::MBS; "Info"; 0; Get(FileName); "InfoTouchBar" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set which button show by default:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.SetDefaultItemIdentifiers"; $t; "AddRecord¶InfoButton¶NSTouchBarItemIdentifierOtherItemsProxy")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set the primary button:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.SetPrincipalItemIdentifier"; $t; "AddRecord" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set which buttons can be configured:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.SetCustomizationAllowedItemIdentifiers"; $t; "AddRecord¶InfoButton¶DeleteRecord")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set this touch bar to be the app one:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.AssignToApp"; $t )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.Available
functions.description: Checks whether TouchBar is available. Returns 1 on macOS 10.12.1 with TouchBar support (or newer). The value 1 does not mean that there is a TouchBar, just the software to handle it. But 0 clearly says that there is none.

examples.label: Check touchbar:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "TouchBar.Available" ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "TouchBar" ; "Is available and may be installed." ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "TouchBar" ; "Is not available." ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "TouchBar.Available" ) ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "TouchBar" ; "Is available and may be installed." ] 
Else
	Show Custom Dialog [ "TouchBar" ; "Is not available." ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.Clear
functions.description: Clears items in this TouchBar.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.Create
functions.description: Creates a new touch bar object. Returns an error if TouchBar is not available.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetAutomaticCustomizeTouchBarMenuItemEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether customize menu is automatically enabled. Whether or not a menu item to customize the touch bar can be automatically added to the main menu. It will only actually be added when a touch bar hardware or simulator is present. Defaults to 0. Setting this property to 1 is the recommended way to add the customization menu item. But if non-standard placement of the menu item is needed, creating a menu item with an action of toggleTouchBarCustomizationPalette can be used instead.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetCustomizationAllowedItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Queries allowed items identifier. The items that are presented in the customization palette for the user to add to the touch bar. These items will be presented to the user in the order specified in this array.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetCustomizationIdentifier
functions.description: Queries the customization identifier. A string uniquely identifying this bar for customization purposes. All bars with this identifier will have their items coordinated automatically during customization or instantiation. Touch bars lacking a customizationIdentifier are not customizable.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetCustomizationRequiredItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Queries the required identifiers. Some items are too important to be removed. The corresponding item identifiers should be listed here. During customization the user will be prevented from removing these items from the touch bar.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetDefaultItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Queries the default identifiers. An array of identifiers specifying the items in this touch bar. Item identifiers should be globally unique, excepting NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFixedSpaceSmall, NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFixedSpaceLarge, NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFlexibleSpace, and NSTouchBarItemIdentifierOtherItemsProxy. This array also corresponds to the item ordering for the receiver in the "default set" in the customization palette.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Queries the resolved array of item identifiers. If the bar has not been customized this will match the defaultItemIdentifiers.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.GetPrincipalItemIdentifier
functions.description: Queries the principal item identifier. Specifying a principal item identifier communicates that the item with that identifier has special significance to this touch bar. Currently, that item will be placed in the center of the resolved touch row. Note that multiple visible bars may each specify a principal item identifier - but only one of them can have the request honored.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of Touch Bar objects. Returns IDs for all TouchBar objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call TouchBar.Release in your scripts.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.Release
functions.description: Releases the TouchBar.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: TouchBar.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Frees all TouchBar objects.

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.SetAutomaticCustomizeTouchBarMenuItemEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether customize menu is automatically enabled. Whether or not a menu item to customize the touch bar can be automatically added to the main menu. It will only actually be added when a touch bar hardware or simulator is present. Defaults to 0. Setting this property to 1 is the recommended way to add the customization menu item. But if non-standard placement of the menu item is needed, creating a menu item with an action of toggleTouchBarCustomizationPalette can be used instead. Causes FileMaker 19 to crash on menu rebuild!

parameters.name: Setting
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Enable menu command:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
TouchBar.Available
" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "TouchBar.SetAutomaticCustomizeTouchBarMenuItemEnabled"; 1 )]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "TouchBar.Available" )]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "TouchBar.SetAutomaticCustomizeTouchBarMenuItemEnabled"; 1 )]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Disable it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.SetAutomaticCustomizeTouchBarMenuItemEnabled"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.SetCustomizationAllowedItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Sets allowed items identifier. The items that are presented in the customization palette for the user to add to the touch bar. These items will be presented to the user in the order specified in this array.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of items.

functions.name: TouchBar.SetCustomizationIdentifier
functions.description: Sets the customization identifier. A string uniquely identifying this bar for customization purposes. All bars with this identifier will have their items coordinated automatically during customization or instantiation. Touch bars lacking a customizationIdentifier are not customizable.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The identifier you want to use.

functions.name: TouchBar.SetCustomizationRequiredItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Sets the required identifiers. Some items are too important to be removed. The corresponding item identifiers should be listed here. During customization the user will be prevented from removing these items from the touch bar.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of items.

functions.name: TouchBar.SetDefaultItemIdentifiers
functions.description: Sets the default identifiers. An array of identifiers specifying the items in this touch bar. Item identifiers should be globally unique, excepting NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFixedSpaceSmall, NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFixedSpaceLarge, NSTouchBarItemIdentifierFlexibleSpace, and NSTouchBarItemIdentifierOtherItemsProxy. This array also corresponds to the item ordering for the receiver in the "default set" in the customization palette.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of items.

examples.label: Sets an item some space and a proxy for others:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.SetPrincipalItemIdentifier
functions.description: Sets the principal item identifier. Specifying a principal item identifier communicates that the item with that identifier has special significance to this touch bar. Currently, that item will be placed in the center of the resolved touch row. Note that multiple visible bars may each specify a principal item identifier - but only one of them can have the request honored.

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the main command.

examples.label: Set main item:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TouchBar.SetPrincipalItemIdentifier"; $t; "NextRecord" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.ToggleCustomizationPalette
functions.description: Show or dismiss the customization palette for the currently displayed touch bars. NSApplication validates this selector against whether the current touch bars are customizable and, if configured on a menu item, will standardize and localize the title. If the current system does not have touch bar support, the menu item will be automatically hidden.

examples.label: Toggle Customization:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
TouchBar.Available
" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("TouchBar.ToggleCustomizationPalette")]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["No TouchBar supported on this Mac."]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "TouchBar.Available" )]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("TouchBar.ToggleCustomizationPalette")]
Else
	Show Custom Dialog ["No TouchBar supported on this Mac."]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TouchBar.Visible
functions.description: Queries visible state. When 1, the touch bar is attached to an eligible touch bar provider, and its items are displayable, assuming adequate space. This function checks for a license. Created 1st November 2016 , last changed 1st November 2016 TouchBar.ToggleCustomizationPalette - Trace 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: TouchBarRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the touch bar.

functions.name: Version
functions.description: Useful for doing auto-updates from server and version checking. e.g. 12.3.0.08 We try to have the build number on the end always use two digits, so it has the zero as prefix if needed. Usually we have 6 big releases per year from .0 to .5. And the first number is basically the year. While development started 2006, the numbering was later adjusted to match year (from 2010) with version 4 in 2014. For automatic checks, please use VersionAutoUpdate function as it always returns an eight digit number.

examples.label: Get the Plugin Version
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Version")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query Plugin version on Server from Client with two scripts:
examples.text_code:
# First a script called "Query
MBS
Version on Server" which you can run:
Perform Script on Server [Wait for completion; “Query
MBS
Version Helper”]
Set Variable [
$e
; Value:Get(LastError)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:Get(ScriptResult)]
If [
$e
≠  0]
Show Custom Dialog ["
MBS
Version on Server"; "Failed to run script on server: " &
$e
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["
MBS
Version on Server";
$r
]
End If
# Second, the helper script called "Query
MBS
Version Helper" which will run on the server:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Version") & " " &
MBS
("Platform")]
Exit Script [Result:
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> First a script called "Query MBS Version on Server" which you can run:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="164" name="Perform Script on Server">
    <WaitForCompletion state="True"/>
    <Script id="99999" name="Query MBS Version Helper"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(LastError)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$e</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptResult)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$e  ≠  0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"MBS Version on Server"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to run script on server: " & $e</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"MBS Version on Server"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Second, the helper script called "Query MBS Version Helper" which will run on the server:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Version") & " " & MBS("Platform")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation>Result: $r</Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.Available
functions.description: Whether OCR is available. Returns 0 on Windows 8.x. Reports 1 on Windows 10240 or later.

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WindowsOCR.Available" ) ]
If [
$r
= 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Available" ]
Else If [
$r
= 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Not available" ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ;
$r
]
End If

functions.name: WindowsOCR.CurrentInputMethodLanguageTag
functions.description: Queries current language tag for the user.

functions.name: WindowsOCR.IsLanguageSupported
functions.description: Whether a language is supported for OCR. Returns 1 if a specified language can be resolved to any of the available OCR languages.

parameters.name: LanguageTag
parameters.description: The language specification. Can be language tag, display name or localized name of the language.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "WindowsOCR.IsLanguageSupported"; "de-DE" ) = 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "We can recognize German text." ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "We can't recognize German text." ]
End If
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

	Show Custom Dialog [ "We can recognize German text." ] 
Else
	Show Custom Dialog [ "We can't recognize German text." ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of OCR engine objects. Returns IDs for all OCR objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call WindowsOCR.Release in your scripts.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.MaxImageDimension
functions.description: Gets the maximum image pixel dimensions supported by the OCR engine. FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 2/2023, Seite 28 This function is free to use. Created 22nd January 2023 , last changed 22nd January 2023 WindowsOCR.List - WindowsOCR.New 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: WindowsOCR.New
functions.description: Creates a new OCR engine. If LanguageTag is empty, we try to use current language from the current user on Windows. If the language can be resolved to any of the OCR languages available on the device, we return a reference number for the OCR engine. Please use WindowsOCR.Release later to free the object.

parameters.name: LanguageTag
parameters.description: The language specification. Can be language tag, display name or localized name of the language. If empty, we use the current user’s language as returned by WindowsOCR.CurrentInputMethodLanguageTag function.

functions.name: WindowsOCR.Recognize
functions.description: Scans the specified image for text. We will ask Windows to load the image from the image container and then run OCR. Seems to work fine for JPEG and PNG files, but other formats may be supported by Windows, too. We built-in support for PDF files to recognize text from first page. See also Vision.RecognizeText function.

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image container to use. Can be JPEG, PNG, BMP, PDF and other data types.

examples.label: Recognize some text from image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ocr
; Value:
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.New
";
Windows OCR::Language
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Failed to intiialize: " &
$ocr
]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WindowsOCR.Recognize";
$ocr
;
Windows OCR::InputImage
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Failed to recognize: " &
$r
]
Else
Set Field [
Windows OCR::Text
;
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.Text
";
$ocr
) ]
End If
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.Release
";
$ocr
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

    <Name>$ocr</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Windows OCR" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to intiialize: " & $ocr </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Recognize"; $ocr; Windows OCR::InputImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Windows OCR" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to recognize: " & $r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Text"; $ocr ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Windows OCR" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Release"; $ocr) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.RecognizeFile
functions.description: Scans the specified image for text. We will ask Windows to load the image from the image file and then run OCR. Windows supports file formats including JPEG, PNG, GIF, TIFF, BMP and HEIF.

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to use.

functions.name: WindowsOCR.RecognizerLanguage
functions.description: Queries the language used for the recognizer. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd January 2023 , last changed 22nd January 2023 WindowsOCR.RecognizeFile - WindowsOCR.Release 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: What value to return. Can be LanguageTag, DisplayName and NativeName.

functions.name: WindowsOCR.Release
functions.description: Releases the object and all memory used for it. Please call this when you are done with an OCR engine.

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

functions.name: WindowsOCR.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Release all our objects. You should better use WindowsOCR.Release with the reference numbers you got earlier as this function releases all objects and that may include objects from other scripts. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.Text
functions.description: Gets the recognized text.

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

examples.label: Query text after running OCR:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ocr
; Value:
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.New
";
Windows OCR::Language
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Failed to intiialize: " &
$ocr
]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.Recognize
";
$ocr
;
Windows OCR::InputImage
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows OCR" ; "Failed to recognize: " &
$r
]
Else
Set Field [
Windows OCR::Text
;
MBS
( "WindowsOCR.Text";
$ocr
) ]
End If
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WindowsOCR.Release
";
$ocr
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.New"; Windows OCR::Language) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ocr</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Windows OCR" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to intiialize: " & $ocr </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Recognize"; $ocr; Windows OCR::InputImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Windows OCR" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to recognize: " & $r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Text"; $ocr ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Windows OCR" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsOCR.Release"; $ocr) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsOCR.TextAngle
functions.description: Gets the clockwise rotation of the recognized text, in degrees, around the center of the image. Can be called after you recognised some text. The clockwise rotation of the recognized text, in degrees, around the center of the image. If the angle of the text can't be detected, the value of this property is null. If the image contains text at different angles, only part of the text will be recognized correctly. Use the TextAngle property to overlay recognition results correctly on the original image. If the value of the TextAngle property is not null or 0 (zero), then to overlay the recognized text correctly on the original image, you either have to rotate the original image by the detected angle in a counter-clockwise direction, or rotate the recognized text by the detected angle in a clockwise direction.

parameters.name: OCR
parameters.description: The reference number of the ocr engine.

functions.name: WinSendMail.AddAttachmentContainer
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a container field. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. For Outlook testing shows only one attachment works in older version, while Thunderbird works with several attachments. This may be fixed for Outlook 365. Outlook also allows to pass no text and a html file as attachment to build a html email.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to attach to email.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, we use file name from container.

examples.label: Adds an image as attachment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.AddAttachmentFile"; $mail; myTable::myImage; "birthday.jpg" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinSendMail.AddAttachmentFile
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a file path. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. For Outlook testing shows only one attachment works in older version, while Thunderbird works with several attachments. This may be fixed for Outlook 365. Outlook also allows to pass no text and a html file as attachment to build a html email.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to include as attachment.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, we use file name from file path.

examples.label: Adds an image as attachment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.AddAttachmentFile"; $mail; "c:\temp\test.jpg"; "birthday.jpg" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinSendMail.AddAttachmentText
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a text. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. For Outlook testing shows only one attachment works in older version, while Thunderbird works with several attachments. This may be fixed for Outlook 365. Outlook also allows to pass no text and a html file as attachment to build a html email.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The text to use for the attachment.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use. Default is attachment.txt

examples.label: Adds an attachment with text field:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.AddAttachmentText"; $Mail; MyTable::MyTextField; "UTF-8" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set HTML for the email:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$EmailID
; Value:
MBS
("
WinSendMail.CreateEmail
") ]
# prepare HTML from field in FileMaker
Set Variable [
$html
; Value: "<html><body>" & GetAsCSS(
EMail::PlainText
) & "</body></html>" ]
# replace a placeholder with a value:
Set Variable [
$html
; Value: Substitute(
$html
; "
$$FirstName
$$";
MBS
( "
Text.EncodeToHTML
";
$FirstName
))]
# now pass the html
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WinSendMail.AddAttachmentText";
$EmailID
;
$html
; "UTF-8"; "test.html") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinSendMail.CreateEmail") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$EmailID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> prepare HTML from field in FileMaker</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"<html><body>" & GetAsCSS(EMail::PlainText) & "</body></html>" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> replace a placeholder with a value:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute( $html; "$$FirstName$$"; MBS( "Text.EncodeToHTML"; $FirstName ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now pass the html</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WinSendMail.AddAttachmentText"; $EmailID; $html; "UTF-8"; "test.html") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinSendMail.AddRecipient
functions.description: Adds a recipient. This function can be used easily if the type is also a field in your database. As an alternative you can of course also use WinSendMail.AddTo , WinSendMail.AddReplyTo, WinSendMail.AddCC or WinSendMail.AddBCC . You can call this method several times to add several recipients. Email addresses need to be prefixed with "SMTP:".

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of recipient. Can be TO, BCC, CC or ReplyTo.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: The email address of the recipient.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the recipient. Not optional when using MAPI with Outlook.

functions.name: WinSendMail.AddTo
functions.description: Adds a recipient. See also WinSendMail.AddRecipient . You can call this method several times to add several recipients. Some mail clients (Outlook) may fail if name is missing. This address is provider-specific message delivery data. Generally, the messaging system provides such addresses for inbound messages. For outbound messages, the Address member can point to an address entered by the user for a recipient not in an address book (that is, a custom recipient). The format of an address pointed to by the Address member is [address type][e-mail address]. Examples of valid addresses are FAX:206-555-1212 and SMTP:M@X.COM.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: The email address of the recipient.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the recipient.

functions.name: WinSendMail.CreateEmail
functions.description: Creates a new email. The reference number returned by this function can be passed to other plugin functions for setting up the email. Later use WinSendMail.Release to free memory. SendMail reference numbers are starting at 67000 and counting up for each new email.

Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Failed to send email"; Message:

      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.SetFrom"; $email; Send::From Email ; Send::From Name ) </Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.SetSubject"; $email; Send::Subject ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.SetText"; $email; Send::Text) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.Send"; $email ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Failed to send email"; Message: $r; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes”</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Commit: “No” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>

  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: WinSendMail.Send
functions.description: Sends email. The email is sent using the locally installed mail client. Some email clients seem not to like more than one attachment. With outlook you can keep text empty and attach a html file. Then Outlook will use the html file as content of the email and allows you to send html email. Falls back to WinSendMail.SendAnsi if Unicode support is missing in older Windows versions. Outlook loves to return failed, if you pass in email address for To/BCC/CC and no name. See WinSendMail.SendAsync for having a function which does not wait.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags. Add 1 to show no dialog. Add 2 to disable logon user interface. Add 4 to disable parent window.

functions.name: WinSendMail.SendAsync
functions.description: Sends email asynchronously. The email is sent using the locally installed mail client. But this function does not wait for the result. Some email clients seem not to like more than one attachment. With outlook you can keep text empty and attach a html file. Then Outlook will use the html file as content of the email and allows you to send html email. Falls back to WinSendMail.SendAnsi if Unicode support is missing in older Windows versions. Outlook loves to return failed, if you pass in email address for To/BCC/CC and no name.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags. Add 1 to show no dialog. Add 2 to disable logon user interface. Add 4 to disable parent window. Add 8 to keep window open and let the user edit the email.

parameters.name: Name

functions.description: Sets the text content. You can use Text.MergeFields to replace placeholders with values from variables, fields and expressions.

parameters.name: WinSendMailRef
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using WinSendMai.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new text.

examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>MBS( "WinSendMail.SetText"; $email; Send::Text) </Calculation>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
